title: Move semantics basics deterministic evidence bank

items.title: Move semantics basics: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this CPP misconception? std::move immediately empties an object and guarantees exactly which value remains in the source.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. std::move only enables move overload selection; after an actual move, rely only on the source being valid unless a stronger state is documented.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. std::move only enables move overload selection; after an actual move, rely only on the source being valid unless a stronger state is documented.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned CPP correction instead of the documented misconception.

options.text: std::move only enables move overload selection; after an actual move, rely only on the source being valid unless a stronger state is documented.
std::move immediately empties an object and guarantees exactly which value remains in the source.

answer.explanation: std::move only enables move overload selection; after an actual move, rely only on the source being valid unless a stronger state is documented.

items.title: Move semantics basics: executable application
items.prompt: Implement move construction for a Token owning an int through unique_ptr, transfer ownership while leaving the source empty, then print empty and the destination value.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent human standards, security, and assessment review.

hints: Begin with the declared input and exact output contract, then implement the named language facet without relying on undefined behavior.

feedback.correct: The reference-aligned visible and hidden deterministic checks passed in the pinned isolated runtime.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic check failed; inspect input validation, boundary behavior, and the named language rule without exposing hidden cases.

criteria.description: Compiles in the pinned runtime and passes every visible and hidden exact-output case.
Uses the target C or C++ language facet with declared bounds and without an out-of-scope workaround.

privateAuthorNotes: AI-assisted and human-unreviewed: formal exam eligibility is prohibited until independent technical and assessment approval.
Reference verification uses the digest-pinned local container with network=none, no package installation, a read-only source mount, and deterministic stdin/stdout.
Executable facet: explicit moved-from state in a move-only owner

answer.explanation: The reference solution exercises explicit moved-from state in a move-only owner under the bounded deterministic contract; it remains an AI-assisted draft pending human review.
